metadata.title: Career Exploration and Goal Setting
metadata.subtitle: Building Your Path to Professional Success

content.title: Career Exploration and Goal Setting
content.subtitle: Building Your Path to Professional Success

content.label: The Challenge
content.question:
What's the difference between working a job and building a career?

How can you take control of your professional future?

objectives.description: Understand the fundamental difference between a job and a career, and why it matters for your future.
Identify your personal interests, skills, and values for informed career exploration.
Use career exploration resources like O*NET, BLS, and informational interviews effectively.
Develop SMART career goals and create a comprehensive career development plan.

terms.definition: A long-term professional journey encompassing multiple jobs, involving growth, development, and advancement over time.
terms.example: Starting as a staff accountant, then senior accountant, manager, and eventually CFO over 20 years

terms.definition: A specific position or role with defined responsibilities, typically performed in exchange for payment.
terms.example: Working as a cashier at a retail store or an administrative assistant at a company

terms.definition: The process of evaluating your own interests, skills, values, and personality traits to guide career decisions.
terms.example: Taking personality tests, listing skills, reflecting on what motivates you

terms.definition: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound objectives for effective planning.
terms.example: "Complete my CPA certification exam within 18 months of graduation"

leftColumn.title: Job
items: May not align with long-term goals

leftPanel.title: Three Key Areas to Explore
paragraphs: 1. Interests: What subjects do you enjoy? What activities make time fly? What problems do you like solving?
2. Skills: What are you naturally good at? What have you learned through education or experience?
3. Values: What matters most to you in work? What kind of environment do you thrive in?

highlightBox.text: Self-awareness is the foundation of smart career planning. Know yourself before exploring careers.

stats.label: More satisfied in careers aligned with interests

infoCard.title: Questions to Ask Yourself:
items: What do I lose track of time doing?
What topics do I enjoy learning about?
What would I do even without pay?
What kind of impact do I want to make?

leftPanel.title: Online Research Tools
paragraphs: O*NET OnLine (onetonline.org): Comprehensive database with skills, education, salary, and outlook for hundreds of occupations.
Bureau of Labor Statistics: Occupational Outlook Handbook with detailed career information and employment projections.
CareerOneStop: Career assessments, training opportunities, and job search resources.

highlightBox.text: Use multiple resources to get a complete picture of potential career paths and their requirements.

layoutData.title: Setting Goals That Actually Work
paragraphs: SMART goals turn vague aspirations into actionable plans:
bulletPoints: Specific: What exactly do you want to accomplish? Who is involved? Why is this important?
Measurable: How will you track progress? What metrics will show you've achieved it?
Achievable: Is this realistic given your resources, time, and current situation?
Relevant: Does this align with your broader career aspirations and personal values?
Time-bound: What is your target date for completion? When will you reach milestones?
layoutData.keyPoint: 💡 SMART goals transform "I want to work in healthcare" into "I will complete my nursing prerequisites with a 3.5 GPA by May 2026."

rightColumn.title: SMART Goal
items: "I will become a registered nurse in pediatric care."
Secure pediatric nursing position by June 2029

layoutData.title: Building Your Roadmap to Success
paragraphs: A comprehensive career development plan includes:
bulletPoints: Career Objective: Your ultimate career direction and aspiration
Education & Training: Degrees, certifications, and courses needed
Experience Building: Internships, jobs, and volunteer work
Skill Development: Technical and soft skills to acquire
Timeline & Milestones: Short, mid, and long-term goals
layoutData.keyPoint: 📋 A career development plan is your personalized roadmap—it turns dreams into actionable steps.

scenario.name: Sarah: From Job to Career
paragraphs: Sarah started as a receptionist at a medical clinic right after high school—viewing it as just a job to pay bills.
After observing nurses and medical assistants, she became interested in healthcare as a career. She created a development plan with short, mid, and long-term goals:
Short-term (1-2 years): Complete nursing prerequisites while continuing receptionist work, save for tuition, gain patient care experience as a medical assistant
Mid-term (3-5 years): Earn Associate's RN degree and license, secure position in pediatric nursing
Long-term (5+ years): Specialize in pediatric oncology, pursue Nurse Practitioner degree, work at children's hospital
Her transformation: A receptionist job became the foundation for a nursing career aligned with her passion for helping children.

scenario.name: Miguel: Career Exploration Process
paragraphs: Miguel, a high school junior interested in technology, wasn't sure which specific career to pursue. He used a systematic exploration process:
Self-assessment: Identified strengths in problem-solving, communication, and creativity; interests in coding, design, and helping others use technology effectively.

Research: Used the Occupational Outlook Handbook to compare Software Developer, UX/UI Designer, IT Support Specialist, and Computer Science Teacher. Analyzed salary ranges, education requirements, and job outlooks.
Informational Interviews: Conversations with professionals in each field revealed that UX/UI design best combined his technical skills with creative interests.
SMART Goals Set: "Complete online UX design course within 6 months" and "Build portfolio with 3 design projects by year end."

content.question:
Why is it important to have both short-term and long-term goals in your career development plan?

How can short-term goals help you stay motivated toward long-term aspirations?

Think about a career that interests you.

What do you already know about it?
What questions do you still need to answer through research?
Who could you talk to for an informational interview?

questions.question: Explain the difference between a job and a career. Why does this distinction matter?
What are the three key areas to explore in a self-assessment, and why is each important?
What does SMART stand for in goal setting? Give an example of transforming a vague goal into a SMART goal.
List three resources you can use for career exploration and what type of information each provides.

takeaways.title: Jobs ≠ Careers
takeaways.description: Jobs provide immediate income; careers offer long-term growth aligned with your goals and values

takeaways.title: Know Yourself First
takeaways.description: Self-assessment of interests, skills, and values is the foundation of smart career planning

takeaways.title: Research Thoroughly
takeaways.description: Use O*NET, BLS, informational interviews, and job shadowing to explore careers

takeaways.title: Plan with SMART Goals
takeaways.description: Create Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound goals with clear milestones

main.title: Career Path Planner
main.description: Use the Career Path Planner skill builder to create your own comprehensive career development plan. This interactive tool will guide you through self-assessment, career research, and goal-setting to build a personalized roadmap for your professional future.

steps: Research 2-3 careers that align with your profile
Write one SMART goal for short-term (1-2 yrs)
Write one SMART goal for long-term (5+ yrs)
Identify education and skills needed for your path

content.tagline: "Your career is a marathon, not a sprint—plan accordingly"
